guarantees.requirement: A count between 0 and the number of participants.
guarantees.backTranslation: The count of free participants at a given slot is between 0 and the total number of participants.

guarantees.requirement: The per-slot count of how many participants are free at each slot.

ensures: forall(s, 0 <= s && s < e.numSlots ==> \result[s] === countFree(e.participants, s))

guarantees.backTranslation: The heatmap function produces an array where each element at slot s equals the count of free participants at that slot, and all values are bounded by the number of participants.

guarantees.requirement: The largest count in the heatmap, and for a non-empty heatmap a value that some slot actually attains.

ensures: h.length > 0 ==> exists(s, 0 <= s && s < h.length && h[s] === \result)

guarantees.backTranslation: The maxCount function returns the maximum value in the heatmap array, and if the array is non-empty, that maximum is actually achieved at some index.

guarantees.requirement: Marks exactly the slots whose free-count ties the maximum, with nothing marked until at least one participant has entered availability.

ensures: forall(s, 0 <= s && s < e.numSlots ==> \result[s] === (heatmap(e)[s] === maxCount(heatmap(e)) && maxCount(heatmap(e)) > 0))

guarantees.backTranslation: The isBest function returns a boolean array where each slot is marked true if and only if it has the maximum count of free participants and that maximum is positive.

guarantees.requirement: Marks exactly the slots where at least k participants are free.
guarantees.backTranslation: The availableAtLeast function returns a boolean array where each slot is marked true if and only if the count of free participants at that slot is at least k.

guarantees.requirement: Counting two participant batches separately and summing equals counting the two concatenated — the free-count is additive over concatenation.

spec.signature: countFreeConcat(xs: Participant[], ys: Participant[], s: number): boolean
ensures: countFree(xs.concat(ys), s) === countFree(xs, s) + countFree(ys, s)

guarantees.backTranslation: The count of free participants at a slot in a concatenated array equals the sum of counts in each array separately.

guarantees.requirement: A slot's free-count is the same whether two participant batches are concatenated in one order or the other.

spec.signature: countFreeComm(xs: Participant[], ys: Participant[], s: number): boolean

guarantees.backTranslation: The count of free participants is invariant under reordering of concatenated arrays.

guarantees.requirement: Two events that differ only in the order of two participant batches have identical heatmaps.

spec.signature: heatmapBatchOrderInvariant(a: Event, b: Event, xs: Participant[], ys: Participant[]): boolean
ensures: forall(s, 0 <= s && s < a.numSlots ==> heatmap(a)[s] === heatmap(b)[s])

guarantees.backTranslation: The heatmap is invariant when participants are reordered by concatenating them in a different order.

guarantees.requirement: A well-formed event of the given grid width; that it starts empty is not part of the proven contract.

spec.signature: initEvent(id: string, title: string, numSlots: number): Event

guarantees.backTranslation: Creating an event with a given number of slots produces a well-formed event with that exact number of slots.

guarantees.requirement: Appending a participant whose row matches the grid width preserves the invariant that every row has the grid width.

spec.signature: allAvailLenSnoc(ps: Participant[], p: Participant, n: number): boolean

guarantees.backTranslation: If all participants in an array have availability arrays of length n, and a new participant has an availability array of length n, then all participants in the concatenated array have availability arrays of length n.

guarantees.requirement: Preserves well-formedness and the grid width; the roster addition itself is not part of the proven contract.
guarantees.backTranslation: Adding a participant with a compatible availability array to a well-formed event produces a well-formed event with the same number of slots.

guarantees.requirement: Replacing one participant's availability keeps every row at the grid width.

spec.signature: setAvailPreservesLen(ps: Participant[], pid: string, newAvail: boolean[], n: number): boolean

guarantees.backTranslation: Setting a participant's availability in an array preserves the invariant that all participants have availability arrays of a fixed length.

guarantees.requirement: Preserves well-formedness and the grid width; the row replacement itself is not part of the proven contract.

spec.signature: setAvailability(e: Event, pid: string, newAvail: boolean[]): Event

guarantees.backTranslation: Setting a participant's availability in a well-formed event produces a well-formed event with the same number of slots.

guarantees.requirement: Removing a participant keeps every remaining row at the grid width.

spec.signature: removePPreservesLen(ps: Participant[], pid: string, n: number): boolean

guarantees.backTranslation: Removing a participant from an array preserves the invariant that all remaining participants have availability arrays of a fixed length.

guarantees.requirement: Preserves well-formedness and the grid width; the removal itself is not part of the proven contract.
guarantees.backTranslation: Removing a participant from a well-formed event produces a well-formed event with the same number of slots.

guarantees.requirement: Appending index y to a sparse list adds exactly y to its membership and changes nothing else.

spec.signature: containsSnoc(xs: number[], y: number, i: number): boolean

guarantees.backTranslation: A number is contained in a concatenated array if and only if it is contained in the original array or equals the appended element.

guarantees.requirement: The indices at which the availability bitset is true.

ensures: forall(i, contains(\result, i) === (0 <= i && i < a.length && a[i]))

guarantees.backTranslation: The sparsify function converts a boolean array to an array of indices where the boolean array is true.

guarantees.requirement: Decodes a sparse index list into a width-n bitset whose bit i is set exactly when i is in the list.

ensures: forall(i, 0 <= i && i < n ==> \result[i] === contains(idxs, i))

guarantees.backTranslation: The densify function converts an array of indices to a boolean array of a given length, where each position is true if its index is in the input array.

guarantees.requirement: Encoding an availability bitset to its sparse true-index list and decoding back reconstructs the original exactly.

ensures: forall(i, 0 <= i && i < a.length ==> densify(sparsify(a), a.length)[i] === a[i])

guarantees.backTranslation: Converting a boolean array to sparse form and back to dense form recovers the original array.

guarantees.requirement: If every participant is free at a slot, the free-count there equals the full roster size.

requires: forall(i, 0 <= i && i < ps.length ==> freeAt(ps[i], s) === true)

guarantees.backTranslation: If all participants are free at a given slot, then the count of free participants at that slot equals the total number of participants.

guarantees.requirement: A participant joining never lowers any slot's free-count.

ensures: forall(s, 0 <= s && s < e.numSlots ==> heatmap(addParticipant(e, p))[s] >= heatmap(e)[s])

guarantees.backTranslation: Adding a participant to an event increases or maintains the count of free participants at each slot.

guarantees.requirement: If everyone is free at a given slot, that slot is among the recommended best slots.

requires: forall(i, 0 <= i && i < e.participants.length ==> freeAt(e.participants[i], s) === true)

guarantees.backTranslation: If all participants are free at a given slot, then that slot is marked as best.

guarantees.requirement: Two last-writer-wins writes to the same participant with distinct timestamps commute — applying them in either order gives the same result.

spec.signature: setAvailLWWCommutes(ps: Participant[], pid: string, a1: boolean[], t1: number, a2: boolean[], t2: number): boolean
ensures: setAvailLWW(setAvailLWW(ps, pid, a1, t1), pid, a2, t2) === setAvailLWW(setAvailLWW(ps, pid, a2, t2), pid, a1, t1)

guarantees.backTranslation: Setting availability with different timestamps commutes: the order of operations does not matter when timestamps differ.

guarantees.requirement: A last-writer-wins write keeps every row at the grid width.

spec.signature: setAvailLWWPreservesLen(ps: Participant[], pid: string, avail: boolean[], at: number, n: number): boolean

guarantees.backTranslation: Setting availability with a timestamp preserves the invariant that all participants have availability arrays of a fixed length.

guarantees.requirement: Preserves well-formedness and the grid width; the last-writer-wins update itself is not part of the proven contract.

spec.signature: setAvailabilityLWW(e: Event, pid: string, avail: boolean[], at: number): Event

guarantees.backTranslation: Setting a participant's availability with a timestamp in a well-formed event produces a well-formed event with the same number of slots.

guarantees.requirement: Preserves the grid width; the op's effect on the roster is not part of the proven contract.
guarantees.backTranslation: Applying an operation to an event preserves the number of slots.

guarantees.requirement: Applying a well-formed op to a well-formed event yields a well-formed event.
guarantees.backTranslation: Applying a valid operation to a well-formed event produces a well-formed event.

guarantees.requirement: Replaying a well-formed op log over a well-formed event yields a well-formed event of the same grid width.
guarantees.backTranslation: Replaying a sequence of valid operations on a well-formed event produces a well-formed event with the same number of slots.

guarantees.requirement: A list whose length equals the slot's free-count.
guarantees.backTranslation: The freeParticipants function returns an array of participants who are free at a given slot, with length equal to the count of free participants.

guarantees.requirement: A list whose size equals the heatmap count for that slot.
guarantees.backTranslation: The whoIsFree function returns an array of participants who are free at a given slot, with length equal to the heatmap value at that slot.

guarantees.requirement: Any permutation of the participant list leaves every slot's free-count unchanged.

spec.signature: countFreePerm(xs: Participant[], ys: Participant[], s: number): boolean

guarantees.backTranslation: The count of free participants at a slot is invariant under permutation of the participant array.

guarantees.requirement: Two events whose participant lists are permutations of each other have identical heatmaps.

ensures: forall(s, 0 <= s && s < a.numSlots ==> heatmap(a)[s] === heatmap(b)[s])

guarantees.backTranslation: The heatmap is invariant when the participant array is permuted.
